REIS IA SURFACES DESIGN SYSTEM v1.0

Surface System

Depth is communicated through 5 tiers of background lightness, not through shadows. Each tier is a step closer to the viewer. Elevation comes from background value, creating a layered visual hierarchy on the dark canvas.

[01] Surface Tiers

5 tiers from pure black (Void) to the lightest elevated layer (Float). Each step increases lightness by a controlled amount, creating perceptible but subtle depth.

S-0
Surface 0 — Void
#000000

The absolute base. Used as the page background on all pages. Nothing sits behind this.

Page background, deepest layer
S-1
Surface 1 — Base
#0A0A0A

Sections alternate between Surface-0 and Surface-1. The lightest page-level background.

Alternating section backgrounds
S-2
Surface 2 — Raised
#111111

The default background for cards, panels, and contained content areas.

Card backgrounds (default)
S-3
Surface 3 — Elevated
#161616

Used for interactive hover states (card hover shifts from S-2 to S-3) and form input backgrounds.

Card hover, input backgrounds
S-4
Surface 4 — Float
#1A1A1A

The topmost layer. Reserved for elements that float above the content plane: dropdowns, tooltips, popovers.

Dropdowns, tooltips, floating elements
Section Alternation Rule

Sections alternate between Surface-0 and Surface-1. Hero always starts on Surface-0. Never jump more than one surface tier between adjacent sections. Cards on Surface-0 sections use Surface-2. Cards on Surface-1 sections also use Surface-2.

[02] Depth Hierarchy — Nested Demo

Surfaces create perceived depth through nesting. Each tier inward brings the element visually closer to the viewer. This demo shows the full depth stack from page background through to a floating tooltip.

Surface 0 — Page
Surface 1 — Section
Surface 2 — Card
Surface 3 — Card Hover / Input
Surface 4 — Tooltip
[03] Text on Surfaces

The opacity-based text system ensures readable contrast on every surface tier. Because text uses white at varying opacities, it automatically harmonizes with any background lightness. This demo shows all 5 text levels on each surface.

Surface 0 — Void #000000
Primary
100%
Secondary
70%
Tertiary
50%
Quaternary
35%
Muted
20%
Surface 1 — Base #0A0A0A
Primary
100%
Secondary
70%
Tertiary
50%
Quaternary
35%
Muted
20%
Surface 2 — Raised #111111
Primary
100%
Secondary
70%
Tertiary
50%
Quaternary
35%
Muted
20%
Surface 3 — Elevated #161616
Primary
100%
Secondary
70%
Tertiary
50%
Quaternary
35%
Muted
20%
Surface 4 — Float #1A1A1A
Primary
100%
Secondary
70%
Tertiary
50%
Quaternary
35%
Muted
20%
[04] Borders on Surfaces

Like text, borders use white at varying opacities and naturally harmonize with all surface tiers. The accent border uses blue at 30% opacity for featured elements.

Surface 0 — Void
Subtle
Default
Visible
Strong
Accent
Surface 1 — Base
Subtle
Default
Visible
Strong
Accent
Surface 2 — Raised
Subtle
Default
Visible
Strong
Accent
Surface 3 — Elevated
Subtle
Default
Visible
Strong
Accent
[05] Surface Usage Patterns

Common patterns showing which surface tiers to use for different UI compositions.

Page Layout
Page bg: S-0
Alt sections: S-0 / S-1
Header: S-1 + blur
Footer: S-1
Card Composition
Card default: S-2
Card hover: S-3
Input inside card: S-3
Nested element: S-3 or S-4
Floating Elements
Dropdown: S-4
Tooltip: S-4
Modal: S-3 or S-4
Popover: S-4
Do
  • Alternate sections between Surface-0 and Surface-1
  • Start hero sections on Surface-0
  • Use Surface-2 for all card backgrounds
  • Shift to Surface-3 on card hover
Don't
  • Jump more than one tier between adjacent elements
  • Put Surface-4 content on a Surface-0 background (skip)
  • Use more than 2 surface tiers on mobile
  • Use shadows instead of surface tiers for elevation
[06] Interactive Surface Demo

Hover over these cards to see the surface transition from Surface-2 to Surface-3. The border also shifts from --border-default to --border-visible. This is the standard card hover behavior across the design system.

01

Card Example 1

Hover to see the surface transition. Background shifts from S-2 to S-3, border from default to visible.

02

Card Example 2

Hover to see the surface transition. Background shifts from S-2 to S-3, border from default to visible.

03

Card Example 3

Hover to see the surface transition. Background shifts from S-2 to S-3, border from default to visible.

[07] CSS Variables Reference
Token
Value
Usage
Surface 0
#000000
Page background (Void)
Surface 1
#0A0A0A
Alternating sections (Base)
Surface 2
#111111
Card backgrounds (Raised)
Surface 3
#161616
Card hover, inputs (Elevated)
Surface 4
#1A1A1A
Dropdowns, tooltips (Float)
